channel.title: ConnectBlue Political Ad Tech News
channel.description: Latest news on political advertising technology, digital campaign strategies, and election ad spending trends

item.title: What Meta, Google and TikTok's ads MCP servers mean for media buyers
item.description: Meta, Google, TikTok, Amazon now offer MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers for ads. AI agents (Claude, ChatGPT) can manage campaigns across all platforms via single interface. Massive operational advantage: unified activation, fewer errors, faster optimization. Campaigns without AI agent integration will lag on execution speed and cross-platform coordination.

item.title: Vulnerable members spend big on official comms
item.description: Competitive House members spending $380K+ on taxpayer-funded official communications (franking) in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026. Rep. April McClain Delaney (D-MD) tops list; practice blurs lines between official and campaign activity. Spending surge signals competitive primary/general races; campaigns must monitor and counter official messaging.
item.ns0:encoded: Competitive House members spending $380K+ on taxpayer-funded official communications (franking) in Q4 2025 and Q1 2026. Rep. April McClain Delaney (D-MD) tops list; practice blurs lines between official and campaign activity. Spending surge signals competitive primary/general races; campaigns must monitor and counter official messaging.

item.title: Tech billionaires are spending unprecedented sums in California races. Experts say it's the tip of the iceberg
item.description: Tech industry pours $200M+ into 2026 California elections: Sergey Brin $82M (billionaire tax), Chris Larsen $26M (via multiple Super PACs targeting legislative races). Fragmented strategy across governor, state legislature, local races. Campaigns competing in California face unprecedented saturation; sophisticated micro-targeting and regional messaging now critical to cut through.
item.ns0:encoded: Tech industry pours $200M+ into 2026 California elections: Sergey Brin $82M (billionaire tax), Chris Larsen $26M (via multiple Super PACs targeting legislative races). Fragmented strategy across governor, state legislature, local races. Campaigns competing in California face unprecedented saturation; sophisticated micro-targeting and regional messaging now critical to cut through.

item.title: Koch-aligned PAC launches first big Senate ad buy
item.description: Americans for Prosperity Action (AFP) opens with $6.3M across six Senate battlegrounds (MI, OH, IA, NC, NH, MT). Early ad spend reveals strategic priorities: cost-of-living messaging dominates; even Trump-won states (MT, OH) seeing heavy investment. Campaigns must re-evaluate spend allocation by Q2.
item.ns0:encoded: Americans for Prosperity Action (AFP) opens with $6.3M across six Senate battlegrounds (MI, OH, IA, NC, NH, MT). Early ad spend reveals strategic priorities: cost-of-living messaging dominates; even Trump-won states (MT, OH) seeing heavy investment. Campaigns must re-evaluate spend allocation by Q2.

item.title: Perion Launches New Advanced Features for Programmatic Digital Out of Home
item.description: Programmatic DOOH becomes AI-native with agentic planning, guaranteed delivery, and integrated measurement. 75-80% of DOOH spend still booked direct; Perion brings it into unified programmatic infrastructure—critical advantage for campaigns scaling reach by November 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: Programmatic DOOH becomes AI-native with agentic planning, guaranteed delivery, and integrated measurement. 75-80% of DOOH spend still booked direct; Perion brings it into unified programmatic infrastructure—critical advantage for campaigns scaling reach by November 2026.

item.title: Millions Pour Into Nevada Primary Ads. A Third of the Money is Untraceable.
item.description: Dark money groups now control $5M+ in Nevada primary ad spend—more than 33% of total airtime budget is untraceable. Service First Fund (Lombardo-affiliated) dominates with $4M spend. Campaigns face unprecedented transparency gaps in swing state ad markets.

item.title: OpenAI Races To Add Conversion-Optimized Campaigns
item.description: OpenAI launches CPA pricing for ChatGPT Ads—campaigns can now optimize conversions directly on the platform with real-time tracking. New revenue channel opens for political advertisers seeking AI-native targeting capabilities.
item.ns0:encoded: OpenAI launches CPA pricing for ChatGPT Ads—campaigns can now optimize conversions directly on the platform with real-time tracking. New revenue channel opens for political advertisers seeking AI-native targeting capabilities.

item.title: Are these AI-generated ads slamming Jennifer Jenkins and Jared Moskowitz the future of campaigning?
item.description: Republican Party of Florida produced AI-generated attack ads in 2 days at minimal cost, vs. traditional ads taking weeks. Spencer Pratt's AI-powered LA mayor campaign reached 22% support tied with incumbent. Production speed advantage shifts power to campaigns with AI content capability—deniable attribution and regulatory gaps create unfair competitive advantage.
item.ns0:encoded: Republican Party of Florida produced AI-generated attack ads in 2 days at minimal cost, vs. traditional ads taking weeks. Spencer Pratt's AI-powered LA mayor campaign reached 22% support tied with incumbent. Production speed advantage shifts power to campaigns with AI content capability—deniable attribution and regulatory gaps create unfair competitive advantage.

item.title: Election threats are focused on campaign systems, not voting machines
item.description: Check Point reports 2026 election threats target campaign email, fundraising platforms, and donor databases—not voting infrastructure. 82% of attacks arrive via email; ActBlue and WinRed password compromises suggest attackers are staging follow-on phishing campaigns against campaign staff. Campaign data breaches will measurably impact Q4 messaging by November.

item.title: Political Ad Spending Nears $4 Billion As California Governor's Race Sets Record
item.description: AdImpact reports $3.9B spent through May with $10.8B projected for full cycle— 47% above 4 years ago. California gubernatorial race alone accounts for $315M, with Tom Steyer spending $201M (two-thirds of state total). Late-cycle media buyers will face 15-25% cost premiums in saturated markets.
item.ns0:encoded: AdImpact reports $3.9B spent through May with $10.8B projected for full cycle— 47% above 4 years ago. California gubernatorial race alone accounts for $315M, with Tom Steyer spending $201M (two-thirds of state total). Late-cycle media buyers will face 15-25% cost premiums in saturated markets.

item.title: OpenAI, AP Political Data-Licensing Deal Signals Possible New Service
item.description: OpenAI and Associated Press expanded their data licensing deal through 2029 to integrate live election data directly into ChatGPT. This signals a fundamental shift: AI models will become primary distribution channels for political information, enabling new voter intelligence and targeting capabilities for campaigns.
item.ns0:encoded: OpenAI and Associated Press expanded their data licensing deal through 2029 to integrate live election data directly into ChatGPT. This signals a fundamental shift: AI models will become primary distribution channels for political information, enabling new voter intelligence and targeting capabilities for campaigns.

item.title: California is drowning in internet campaign 'slop.' 2028 is next.
item.description: AI-generated ads and influencer payments are flooding California primaries—with 2-day production timelines replacing traditional weeks-long processes. Campaigns without AI content capability face structural disadvantage by Q3 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: AI-generated ads and influencer payments are flooding California primaries—with 2-day production timelines replacing traditional weeks-long processes. Campaigns without AI content capability face structural disadvantage by Q3 2026.

item.title: Texas population boom, Latino backlash scramble 2026 Senate race
item.description: 2.5M new Texas residents since 2020 with unpredictable voting; Latino disapproval of Trump at 67% (vs 55% support in 2024). Campaigns must rebuild voter models immediately--old data will waste 20-30% of media spend on dead audiences.

item.title: LA mayors race: AI videos supporting Spencer Pratt shake up election
item.description: Viral AI-generated videos created by supporters reach millions organically. Campaigns can leverage user-generated synthetic media without formal spend--grassroots AI content at scale is outperforming traditional paid media in 2026.

item.title: Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: This Is a War
item.description: Competing AI-backed super PACs (Anthropic vs OpenAI) now spend millions to influence 2026 elections. Campaigns must understand AI companies as new power players with policy agendas--vendor consolidation creates asymmetric advantages for aligned campaigns.

item.title: Late surge in CA-48 Super PAC money spend against Campa-Najjar
item.description: Over 3.4M in independent expenditure spending floods CA-48 race
item.ns0:encoded: Over 3.4M in independent expenditure spending floods CA-48 race

item.title: New ad campaign hits Dems on crypto bill
item.description: New Democracy executes six-figure multi-channel media buy in 11 swing states
item.ns0:encoded: New Democracy executes six-figure multi-channel media buy in 11 swing states

item.title: USPS seeks mail-in voter lists from states after Trump order
item.description: Federal USPS requirement for centralized voter data creates new compliance implications for campaigns.

item.title: OpenX Powers Political Reach With Preferred Access to Quality Direct Media for 2026 Midterms
item.description: OpenX and Givsly partnership unlocks values-based voter targeting across CTV with ZIP-code precision in under 24 hours. Industry-first: campaigns match voter values against 500+ nonprofit behavioral data. CTV activation speed increases 10x, costs drop for down-ballot campaigns, and values-based reach becomes alternative to voter file targeting.

item.title: Winning Down-Ballot in 2026
item.description: Down-ballot races now represent 50% of political ad spend and grow faster than federal races. Early-cycle spend arrives 4-6 weeks ahead of historical timelines. CTV is foundational to down-ballot strategy. Campaigns identifying demand early win against competitors using legacy playbooks.

item.title: The Persuasion Gap in Political Advertising
item.description: Campaign targeting is split between mobilization and persuasion. Issue-engaged voters signal higher persuadability. For 2026, campaigns using values-based signals with voter files unlock efficiency gains separating winners from laggards in swing districts.

item.title: Attack ads are reaching record levels in the 2026 race
item.description: Attack ads now comprise 72% of campaign ad budgets in 2026—a record-breaking increase from 2022. This spending surge reflects intensified competitive battles and signals dramatic shifts in campaign strategy and media buying priorities.

item.title: Can AI Improve Trust Between Political Campaigns And Voters?
item.description: Convos, a new platform co-founded by Tom Carroll, is rethinking political SMS communications with AI-powered conversational intelligence. The platform manages voter reply streams, prioritizes interactions, and enables genuine two-way engagement at scale—shifting SMS from broadcast to conversation.

item.title: Inside the Democratic resistance on AI
item.description: Progressive Democrats (Sanders, AOC, Khanna, Warren) are mounting coordinated opposition to AI deployment, data centers, and AI-backed Super PACs. This represents a major policy shift that will directly restrict funding mechanisms and technology choices for 2026 campaigns.

item.title: Powerful A.I. Super PACs Duel Over the Midterms: 'This Is a War'
item.description: Anthropic-backed and OpenAI-backed Super PACs are spending millions on rival campaign operations in 2026 midterms. This represents a fundamental shift in how tech companies compete through political spending—and a new category of political ad buyer for campaigns to navigate.

item.title: ICE's call for data is stirring advertisers to push for privacy protections
item.description: ICE is seeking to purchase commercial adtech data for immigration enforcement investigations—prompting industry-wide alarm over data misuse and regulatory backlash. Campaigns must prepare for stricter data handling compliance and potential restrictions on audience data availability.
item.ns0:encoded: ICE is seeking to purchase commercial adtech data for immigration enforcement investigations—prompting industry-wide alarm over data misuse and regulatory backlash. Campaigns must prepare for stricter data handling compliance and potential restrictions on audience data availability.

item.title: Bionic Adds 66 More Integrations to its Media Buying System—Now 130+ Platforms
item.description: Bionic expanded to 130+ platform integrations via Supermetrics partnership, enabling agencies to centralize media planning data from Amazon DSP, Google DV360, The Trade Desk, Meta, TikTok, YouTube and all major platforms. Automated nightly data pulls eliminate manual spreadsheets. Real-time pacing and optimization mean budget adjustments in hours, not weeks. Competitive advantage: daily performance monitoring.
item.ns0:encoded: Bionic expanded to 130+ platform integrations via Supermetrics partnership, enabling agencies to centralize media planning data from Amazon DSP, Google DV360, The Trade Desk, Meta, TikTok, YouTube and all major platforms. Automated nightly data pulls eliminate manual spreadsheets. Real-time pacing and optimization mean budget adjustments in hours, not weeks. Competitive advantage: daily performance monitoring.

item.title: Ohio voters will see more AI political content this election cycle
item.description: AI deepfakes are now standard-issue attack ads in Ohio's 2026 primary. Video deepfakes are indistinguishable from authentic content without specialized detection software. Campaigns must plan for AI-generated attack messaging and voter education on spotting fabricated content is becoming a competitive factor. Legal/regulatory framework remains undefined at federal level.

item.title: Steven's Newsletter - May 26, 2026: CTV Upfront Tipping Point and the Reality of Agentic Advertising
item.description: Connected TV upfront dollars now exceed linear TV for the first time—CTV upfronts are the only growing channel. Campaigns without scaled CTV by Q3 face inventory premiums. Autonomous ad buying only works for 10-20% of campaigns; complex political media plans still require human strategists. This is the structural shift separating 2026 winners from laggards.
item.ns0:encoded: Connected TV upfront dollars now exceed linear TV for the first time—CTV upfronts are the only growing channel. Campaigns without scaled CTV by Q3 face inventory premiums. Autonomous ad buying only works for 10-20% of campaigns; complex political media plans still require human strategists. This is the structural shift separating 2026 winners from laggards.

item.title: California Influencer Disclosures Reveal Hidden Political Spending—$400K Payments with No Disclosure in Federal Races
item.description: Tom Steyer's California gubernatorial campaign paid influencers $10–$400K+ for political promotion with minimal disclosure. Semafor reports $400K to a single creator, $25K payments to accounts with millions of followers. Federal campaigns still have no influencer spending disclosure requirements—2026 will test enforcement and compliance gaps.
item.ns0:encoded: Tom Steyer's California gubernatorial campaign paid influencers $10–$400K+ for political promotion with minimal disclosure. Semafor reports $400K to a single creator, $25K payments to accounts with millions of followers. Federal campaigns still have no influencer spending disclosure requirements—2026 will test enforcement and compliance gaps.

item.title: Swing Voter Targeting Is Shifting from Demographics to Fandom—Campaigns Are Just Catching Up
item.description: Fandom data (influencer communities, shared identity around creators) is now a primary voter targeting dimension. 21% of Americans get news from social media influencers; fandoms function as built-in distribution networks. Campaigns treating voters as demographics will underperform those activating fandom-based communities.
item.ns0:encoded: Fandom data (influencer communities, shared identity around creators) is now a primary voter targeting dimension. 21% of Americans get news from social media influencers; fandoms function as built-in distribution networks. Campaigns treating voters as demographics will underperform those activating fandom-based communities.

item.title: Samba TV's Project Gravity Solves the Walled-Garden Problem: Real-Time Identity Across 40+ DSPs
item.description: Samba TV launches Project Gravity—unified voter/consumer identity resolution that activates across 40+ DSPs without platform lock-in. Uses AI vector embedding to match offline voter lists to digital profiles in real-time. Campaigns eliminate data silos and platform-based optimization bias.
item.ns0:encoded: Samba TV launches Project Gravity—unified voter/consumer identity resolution that activates across 40+ DSPs without platform lock-in. Uses AI vector embedding to match offline voter lists to digital profiles in real-time. Campaigns eliminate data silos and platform-based optimization bias.

item.title: Sabio Q1 2026: Programmatic Revenue Grows 1,174% YoY—Political & Advocacy Commitments Hit $5M+
item.description: Sabio secured $5M+ in political and advocacy commitments as programmatic CTV revenues explode. Core business grew 6% YoY; international and programmatic combined represent 62% of Q1 revenue. Early signal of election cycle acceleration.

item.title: Programmatic Audio Expands Advertiser Reach — The Basis & DAX Partnership Unlocks Voter Targeting at Scale
item.description: Basis and DAX integrate programmatic audio with 60+ political audience datasets—campaigns now activate voter-targeted audio at scale. Audio reaches 80% of adults daily; campaigns ignoring it leave efficiency on the table by Q3 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: Basis and DAX integrate programmatic audio with 60+ political audience datasets—campaigns now activate voter-targeted audio at scale. Audio reaches 80% of adults daily; campaigns ignoring it leave efficiency on the table by Q3 2026.

item.title: Deep Sync Expands Partnership with MiQ to Power Identity-Driven Political Advertising
item.description: Voter data to digital activation in hours instead of weeks: Deep Sync + MiQ partnership enables campaigns to match voter files to addressable audiences with ~25% addressability improvement on average (~120% on live campaigns), then activate through MiQ programmatic in under 48 hours. Match rates and speed are now competitive advantages.
item.ns0:encoded: Voter data to digital activation in hours instead of weeks: Deep Sync + MiQ partnership enables campaigns to match voter files to addressable audiences with ~25% addressability improvement on average (~120% on live campaigns), then activate through MiQ programmatic in under 48 hours. Match rates and speed are now competitive advantages.

item.title: How to Evaluate an Advertising Platform for Political Campaigns in 2026
item.description: Platform selection now determines campaign speed and efficiency in 2026—48% of budgets run in final 30 days. Omnichannel activation (CTV, audio, display, social, search) from unified workflows eliminates manual handoffs and saves 51 hours per campaign. Political-specific targeting (congressional districts, voter data integrations) and rapid activation separate winners from laggards.
item.ns0:encoded: Platform selection now determines campaign speed and efficiency in 2026—48% of budgets run in final 30 days. Omnichannel activation (CTV, audio, display, social, search) from unified workflows eliminates manual handoffs and saves 51 hours per campaign. Political-specific targeting (congressional districts, voter data integrations) and rapid activation separate winners from laggards.

item.title: Video Ad Spending to Set Record for a Midterm Cycle in 2026
item.description: CTV is the only video channel gaining budget share in 2026—projected at 16% growth vs. 2% decline for local broadcast. Campaigns that scale CTV by Q3 avoid October inventory premiums and CPM spikes that nearly double heading into Election Day.
item.ns0:encoded: CTV is the only video channel gaining budget share in 2026—projected at 16% growth vs. 2% decline for local broadcast. Campaigns that scale CTV by Q3 avoid October inventory premiums and CPM spikes that nearly double heading into Election Day.

item.title: Influencer Spending Exploding in Campaigns—But Compliance Rules Haven't Caught Up
item.description: California's governor race reveals influencer-paid-content gap: campaigns paying $10-$400K per influencer endorsement with vague disclosure rules and no unified federal framework. Influencer networks now core spend category with regulatory risk—FEC/FTC enforcement coming in 2027.
item.ns0:encoded: California's governor race reveals influencer-paid-content gap: campaigns paying $10-$400K per influencer endorsement with vague disclosure rules and no unified federal framework. Influencer networks now core spend category with regulatory risk—FEC/FTC enforcement coming in 2027.

item.title: LinkedIn Kills "AI Slop"—Volume-Based Content Strategies Now Underperform
item.description: LinkedIn algorithm is actively suppressing AI-generated content without genuine expertise. Authentic thought leadership now wins distribution advantage. Campaigns running volume-based content strategies powered by AI will lose 40-60% reach; strategic pivot to organizer voices required.
item.ns0:encoded: LinkedIn algorithm is actively suppressing AI-generated content without genuine expertise. Authentic thought leadership now wins distribution advantage. Campaigns running volume-based content strategies powered by AI will lose 40-60% reach; strategic pivot to organizer voices required.

item.title: Federal AI Review Plans Could Reshape Campaign Strategy in 2026
item.description: White House federal AI governance framework will force campaign tech compliance overhaul—mandatory federal review replaces state-by-state patchwork, threatening retroactive compliance costs for existing AI tools. Campaigns must audit vendor roadmaps by Q3 to avoid mid-cycle workflow rebuilds.

item.title: Upfronts 2026: Ad Tech Takes Center Stage
item.description: Streaming platforms (Netflix, Disney, Amazon, Warner Bros.) led 2026 Upfronts with ad-tech, not content. Netflix 60% ad-tier adoption. Amazon reaches 90% of US households with authenticated signals. CTV+AI optimization now structural advantage; campaigns with programmatic precision beat linear-TV-only buyers 2-3x on efficiency.
item.ns0:encoded: Streaming platforms (Netflix, Disney, Amazon, Warner Bros.) led 2026 Upfronts with ad-tech, not content. Netflix 60% ad-tier adoption. Amazon reaches 90% of US households with authenticated signals. CTV+AI optimization now structural advantage; campaigns with programmatic precision beat linear-TV-only buyers 2-3x on efficiency.

item.title: How Political Advertising Will Impact the Media Landscape in 2026
item.description: CTV is now the ONLY channel growing in political spend ($2.4B vs. $2.34B 2024). Political dollars concentrate in 5 states. Campaigns without early PMP/PG inventory locks by June face $0.12–$0.18 CPM premiums vs. early movers. Deepfake/AI brand safety risk escalating.
item.ns0:encoded: CTV is now the ONLY channel growing in political spend ($2.4B vs. $2.34B 2024). Political dollars concentrate in 5 states. Campaigns without early PMP/PG inventory locks by June face $0.12–$0.18 CPM premiums vs. early movers. Deepfake/AI brand safety risk escalating.

item.title: Democrats eye 2027 crackdown on AI in election ads
item.description: AI deepfakes are flooding 2026 campaigns with zero disclosure requirements—House Democrats plan 2027 regulation. Smart campaigns disclose voluntary; vendors must build compliance tooling now to retain clients post-election.

item.title: Stop Wasting Ad Dollars: How Social Optimization Changes the Math
item.description: 36.5% of programmatic social spend is wasted on unsuitable content impressions. Leading brands combining contextual suitability with platform filters achieved 29% higher engagement, 71% fewer suitability failures, and 18% lower CPMs. Campaigns spending $10M+ in social can recapture hundreds of thousands in waste by shifting to quality-optimized buying.
item.ns0:encoded: 36.5% of programmatic social spend is wasted on unsuitable content impressions. Leading brands combining contextual suitability with platform filters achieved 29% higher engagement, 71% fewer suitability failures, and 18% lower CPMs. Campaigns spending $10M+ in social can recapture hundreds of thousands in waste by shifting to quality-optimized buying.

item.title: DoubleVerify Delivers Global Media Quality Measurement for LinkedIn Audience Network
item.description: DoubleVerify brings third-party media quality verification to LinkedIn Audience Network—enabling real-time measurement of invalid traffic, viewability, brand suitability, and geography. LinkedIn LAN delivers 3.9x more impressions than feed-only. Verified, quality-measured inventory is becoming table stakes for political campaigns.
item.ns0:encoded: DoubleVerify brings third-party media quality verification to LinkedIn Audience Network—enabling real-time measurement of invalid traffic, viewability, brand suitability, and geography. LinkedIn LAN delivers 3.9x more impressions than feed-only. Verified, quality-measured inventory is becoming table stakes for political campaigns.

item.title: Study finds AI chatbots can shape and distort voters' understanding of elections
item.description: Research from UCD and University of Strathclyde reveals AI chatbots (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Grok) provide incomplete, biased, and sometimes fabricated election information. ChatGPT omitted candidates, Gemini showed wrong results. Campaigns must understand this voter information gap affects perception and turnout.

item.title: Broadcast Is Losing Ad Dollars, Despite Massive Reach
item.description: The intelligence infrastructure gap is costing broadcasters $233M in political ad revenue—while programmatic platforms win by proving performance in real-time. Campaigns now demand live data, not quarterly reports. Broadcasters with real-time audience intelligence will win 2026 budgets; those without will lose to faster platforms.
item.ns0:encoded: The intelligence infrastructure gap is costing broadcasters $233M in political ad revenue—while programmatic platforms win by proving performance in real-time. Campaigns now demand live data, not quarterly reports. Broadcasters with real-time audience intelligence will win 2026 budgets; those without will lose to faster platforms.

item.title: Why Political Advertisers Can't Afford Unverified Reach In 2026
item.description: Probabilistic identity doesn't scale for political campaigns operating 30-day windows with FEC disclosure requirements. Unverified reach introduces compliance risk, performance waste, and slower tactical optimization. Authenticated inventory commands premium but ensures certainty and campaign velocity.

item.title: Ampersand Unveils TrueStream Political: Authenticated CTV Inventory at Scale
item.description: Ampersand launches TrueStream Political with deterministic household-level identity from Comcast, Charter, Cox (120M households). Fraud-free premium inventory addresses identity accuracy variance crisis (20-30% variance across platforms) plaguing CTV political buys.

item.title: Viant and Ad Fontes Media Bring Political Bias-Based Targeting to CTV News
item.description: First DSP enabling news reliability-based targeting in CTV. Campaigns can now target voters based on news environment consumed (mainstream, conservative, entertainment-aligned). Reach swing voters in mainstream environments or base voters in conservative environments with tailored messaging.

item.title: Digital Remedy Expands Political Media Offering With Launch Of The Political Desk
item.description: Digital Remedy launches The Political Desk with VoterReach district-level measurement. Campaigns get unified view across CTV/video/display/audio/DOOH plus real-time frequency detection and budget reallocation. Launches in hours, not days—critical speed advantage for 2026.

item.title: Local TV faces financial pinch as political ad spending increasingly moves online
item.description: Broadcast TV political spend drops 7% while online jumps 35%; online now captures 50% of all political ad spend for first time in midterm cycle. Campaigns must shift allocation by Q3 or face premium CPM rates and shrinking inventory.

item.title: A new generation of ads for the AI era of Search
item.description: Google launched Conversational Discovery ads and Highlighted Answers powered by Gemini in Search—allowing campaigns to generate custom product explainers in real-time based on voter queries. Political media buyers gain new demand-capture inventory at scale.
item.ns0:encoded: Google launched Conversational Discovery ads and Highlighted Answers powered by Gemini in Search—allowing campaigns to generate custom product explainers in real-time based on voter queries. Political media buyers gain new demand-capture inventory at scale.

item.title: How AIPAC channels millions through shell PACs ahead of US elections
item.description: Shell PAC structures are proliferating as major groups hide spending sources—layered PAC-to-PAC funding is becoming the primary strategy to obscure donor origins. Political operatives expect increased transparency pressure and FEC enforcement scrutiny on opaque funding chains.

item.title: Democrats eye 2027 crackdown on AI in election ads
item.description: AI-generated political ads are proliferating without disclosure—Democrats plan mandatory AI disclaimer legislation if they retake power. Campaigns must prepare compliance frameworks and audit trails for AI-created content by 2027 or face regulatory exposure.

item.title: Can you trust that post about Tom Steyer? How paid influencers are flooding into the governor's race
item.description: Billionaire Steyer's campaign pays influencers $123K+ to post pro-campaign content; some posts lack required disclosure. Signals influencer content at scale becoming standard political tactic, but with disclosure/transparency vulnerabilities. Campaigns testing paid creator networks to build grassroots appearance.
item.ns0:encoded: Billionaire Steyer's campaign pays influencers $123K+ to post pro-campaign content; some posts lack required disclosure. Signals influencer content at scale becoming standard political tactic, but with disclosure/transparency vulnerabilities. Campaigns testing paid creator networks to build grassroots appearance.

item.title: U.S. Digital Video Ad Spend Projected to Surpass $80B in 2026, Growing 11% YoY
item.description: Digital video ad spending (CTV, social video, YouTube) hits $81.9B in 2026, growing 11% YoY—nearly 20% faster than overall ad market. Political campaigns must prioritize video channels where growth outpaces traditional media; CTV and streaming now mandatory for efficient reach.
item.ns0:encoded: Digital video ad spending (CTV, social video, YouTube) hits $81.9B in 2026, growing 11% YoY—nearly 20% faster than overall ad market. Political campaigns must prioritize video channels where growth outpaces traditional media; CTV and streaming now mandatory for efficient reach.

item.title: Midterm Political Ad Spend Projected To Hit $13.8B, Up 9%
item.description: 2026 political ad spend reaches $13.8B (+9% vs 2022), but digital now commands 50% of budget (up from 46%), while local TV falls to 36% (from 40%). This marks structural shift away from traditional broadcast toward digital, social, and programmatic channels.
item.ns0:encoded: 2026 political ad spend reaches $13.8B (+9% vs 2022), but digital now commands 50% of budget (up from 46%), while local TV falls to 36% (from 40%). This marks structural shift away from traditional broadcast toward digital, social, and programmatic channels.

item.title: Trump got his revenge on Thomas Massie — but at a huge price
item.description: Kentucky's 4th House primary becomes most expensive in U.S. history at $32.6M, with $14M from outside groups. Signals unprecedented primary-cycle spending arms races; campaigns must prepare for massive GOP internal battles funded by Super PACs and Trump-aligned organizations.

item.title: Democrats eye 2027 crackdown on AI in election ads
item.description: House Democrats planning 2027 legislation to regulate AI-generated political ads. AI usage exploded in 2026 (deepfakes, synthetic videos). Rep. Joe Morelle pushing disclosure requirements. Strategic implication: campaigns using undisclosed AI ads without disclosure risk legal exposure post-election. Early transparency now = competitive advantage and regulatory shield.

item.title: Viant and Ad Fontes Media Bring Political Bias-Based Targeting to CTV News
item.description: Viant (DSP) and Ad Fontes Media enable first-to-market news reliability-based targeting on CTV. Political campaigns can now reach voters by news source quality/bias while achieving 60% lower CPA and 50%+ higher conversions. Ads in news environments drive 77% higher brand recall—the efficiency edge every 2026 campaign needs.

item.title: Reddit Expands First-Party Measurement with Dual Attribution
item.description: Reddit launches Dual Attribution into beta—giving political campaigns independent performance measurement on the platform without relying on third-party MMPs. Redditors have 65% higher lifetime value than other social platforms, and political advertisers on Reddit grew installs 129% YoY. Now campaigns can optimize spend and measure impact in real-time.
item.ns0:encoded: Reddit launches Dual Attribution into beta—giving political campaigns independent performance measurement on the platform without relying on third-party MMPs. Redditors have 65% higher lifetime value than other social platforms, and political advertisers on Reddit grew installs 129% YoY. Now campaigns can optimize spend and measure impact in real-time.

item.title: Publicis Acquires LiveRamp for $2.2B: Data Neutrality Ends, Political Campaigns Face Vendor Consolidation Risk
item.description: $2.2B Publicis/LiveRamp acquisition consolidates 'neutral' identity infrastructure under holding company. Campaigns' data now flows through vendor that also sells to competing advertisers. 12–18 month window to diversify away from single-vendor data dependency before margin pressure and further consolidation.

item.title: Midterm Elections Looming: Political Influencer Spending Explodes With Zero FEC/FTC Disclosure
item.description: Gen Z campaigns discovering creator content as high-ROI channel with NO disclosure requirements. Campaigns paying creators $100K+ for political endorsements with zero transparency—regulatory window closing by Q3 2026. Build creator rosters NOW before Q3 compliance frameworks tighten.
item.ns0:encoded: Gen Z campaigns discovering creator content as high-ROI channel with NO disclosure requirements. Campaigns paying creators $100K+ for political endorsements with zero transparency—regulatory window closing by Q3 2026. Build creator rosters NOW before Q3 compliance frameworks tighten.

item.title: Texting Platform Hustle Has New Owners: Its Employees
item.description: Hustle shifts to employee ownership with new 'Win The Future' pay-as-you-go pricing for 2026 campaigns. Mission-aligned ownership creates incentive alignment and lower cost access to enterprise P2P + broadcast + video texting for down-ballot campaigns.

item.title: Voter Intelligence at the Speed of Media: How Aristotle and Always On Data Are Powering the 2026 Cycle
item.description: Voter intelligence now activates across all major DSPs in hours, not weeks—campaigns move from strategy to live targeting without custom integrations. Unified activation-ready data is now table stakes for 2026 media planning.

item.title: DSPolitical and FreeWheel Unlock Scalable, High-Performance CTV Ahead of 2026 Midterm Elections
item.description: Deterministic identity unlocks 18x higher CTV match rates for political campaigns—moving from weeks to hours for voter data activation. This is the competitive advantage separating winners from laggards in 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: Deterministic identity unlocks 18x higher CTV match rates for political campaigns—moving from weeks to hours for voter data activation. This is the competitive advantage separating winners from laggards in 2026.

item.title: Influencer boost budgets are throwing gas on social video spending fire
item.description: Influencer campaign budgets now split 60-40 toward paid amplification (up from afterthought status). Social video spend growing 13% YoY with amplification—but campaigns running legacy organic-only models are leaving 30-50% reach on table. Restructure influencer allocations by June to optimize unit economics by November 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: Influencer campaign budgets now split 60-40 toward paid amplification (up from afterthought status). Social video spend growing 13% YoY with amplification—but campaigns running legacy organic-only models are leaving 30-50% reach on table. Restructure influencer allocations by June to optimize unit economics by November 2026.

item.title: YouTube Rolls Out Likeness Detection To All Creators Over 18
item.description: YouTube's likeness detection now available to all creators globally—enables removal of AI-generated content using someone's face with zero appeal process. Campaigns without AI-content vetting built into production workflows by Q2 will face mid-cycle removal risk and approval delays in Q4. This is a structural shift in video enforcement.
item.ns0:encoded: YouTube's likeness detection now available to all creators globally—enables removal of AI-generated content using someone's face with zero appeal process. Campaigns without AI-content vetting built into production workflows by Q2 will face mid-cycle removal risk and approval delays in Q4. This is a structural shift in video enforcement.

item.title: ActBlue CEO Agrees to Appear Before Congressional Investigators
item.description: ActBlue CEO testifies before House on June 10 over foreign donor vetting—potential platform restrictions could slow Democratic mid-cycle fundraising 15-20%, forcing campaigns to deploy contingency platforms. Q3/Q4 funding velocity is now a political variable.
item.ns0:encoded: ActBlue CEO testifies before House on June 10 over foreign donor vetting—potential platform restrictions could slow Democratic mid-cycle fundraising 15-20%, forcing campaigns to deploy contingency platforms. Q3/Q4 funding velocity is now a political variable.

item.title: Why Ohio Doesn't Have Any Artificial Intelligence Regulations
item.description: AI-generated political ads (deepfakes, doctored images) are running without disclaimers in Ohio primaries. No state AI ad regulations exist despite bipartisan bills introduced. Compliance risk is asymmetric: campaigns using AI face unpredictable enforcement, while opponents' AI content skirts legality. Regulatory landscape will fragment by Q3—campaigns must audit creative production now.
item.ns0:encoded: AI-generated political ads (deepfakes, doctored images) are running without disclaimers in Ohio primaries. No state AI ad regulations exist despite bipartisan bills introduced. Compliance risk is asymmetric: campaigns using AI face unpredictable enforcement, while opponents' AI content skirts legality. Regulatory landscape will fragment by Q3—campaigns must audit creative production now.

item.title: Who's Spending in Your Congressional Election? We Tracked the Front Groups Fueling the 2026 Midterms
item.description: Crypto (Fairshake, $M+ spend), AI (Leading the Future, Anthropic), and AIPAC are deploying "pop-up" PACs and party-affiliate structures to hide funding sources. Campaigns must audit their opposition intelligence and understand what dark money is actually targeting their districts—FEC disclosure deadlines mean voters won't know sources until months after the election.
item.ns0:encoded: Crypto (Fairshake, $M+ spend), AI (Leading the Future, Anthropic), and AIPAC are deploying "pop-up" PACs and party-affiliate structures to hide funding sources. Campaigns must audit their opposition intelligence and understand what dark money is actually targeting their districts—FEC disclosure deadlines mean voters won't know sources until months after the election.

item.title: Basis and DAX United States Partner to Give Political Advertisers Exclusive Access to Premium Audio Inventory
item.description: Audio now captures 80% of daily ad-supported listening with 100M+ accessible listeners through DAX + Basis integration. Political campaigns can programmatically buy podcasts and streaming radio with L2/Experian voter targeting—closing a major inventory gap. Campaigns must scale audio by Q2 to avoid cost premiums in primary stretch.
item.ns0:encoded: Audio now captures 80% of daily ad-supported listening with 100M+ accessible listeners through DAX + Basis integration. Political campaigns can programmatically buy podcasts and streaming radio with L2/Experian voter targeting—closing a major inventory gap. Campaigns must scale audio by Q2 to avoid cost premiums in primary stretch.

item.title: Sell-Side Curation Just Unlocked Value-Aligned Swing Voter Targeting at Scale
item.description: OpenX + Givsly launch values-based voter targeting; campaigns reach voters via nonprofit/cause affiliation (not party); curated deal structures reduce 20-30% identity mismatch waste and expand swing voter pools across all DSPs
item.ns0:encoded: OpenX + Givsly launch values-based voter targeting; campaigns reach voters via nonprofit/cause affiliation (not party); curated deal structures reduce 20-30% identity mismatch waste and expand swing voter pools across all DSPs

item.title: CTV Is Now the Mass-Reach Playbook—Broadcast Becomes the Supplementary Channel
item.description: CTV projected at $2.5B (only growing channel) in 2026 political spend; 44% of swing voters stream-only; cord-cutting now 47.5% of TV viewing; campaigns that fund CTV first will measurably outperform broadcast-anchored campaigns by Q2 2026
item.ns0:encoded: CTV projected at $2.5B (only growing channel) in 2026 political spend; 44% of swing voters stream-only; cord-cutting now 47.5% of TV viewing; campaigns that fund CTV first will measurably outperform broadcast-anchored campaigns by Q2 2026

item.title: $10.8 Billion Political Ad Spend Is Arriving—And CTV CPM Premiums Start Now
item.description: $10.8B political ad spend for 2026 midterms (+20% vs 2022); 50% runs in final 30 days; CTV only growing channel at $2.4B; 5 battleground states (CA, MI, GA, NC, TX) drive inventory scarcity; campaigns must lock in PMP/PG deals by Labor Day to avoid peak pricing
item.ns0:encoded: $10.8B political ad spend for 2026 midterms (+20% vs 2022); 50% runs in final 30 days; CTV only growing channel at $2.4B; 5 battleground states (CA, MI, GA, NC, TX) drive inventory scarcity; campaigns must lock in PMP/PG deals by Labor Day to avoid peak pricing

item.title: 30-State AI Political Ad Compliance Patchwork Is Now Binding Framework for 2026
item.description: 30 US states have enacted AI political ad disclosure laws covering 46% of US voters; 5 distinct regulatory models require per-state creative variations and disclosure templates; federal preemption unlikely but possible through 2027
item.ns0:encoded: 30 US states have enacted AI political ad disclosure laws covering 46% of US voters; 5 distinct regulatory models require per-state creative variations and disclosure templates; federal preemption unlikely but possible through 2027

item.title: Spotify Debuts Generative AI Ads, Programmatic Ad Buying
item.description: Spotify launching AI ad creation tools and expanded programmatic buying for audio, podcasts, and video. New scalable audio channel for 2026 campaigns with AI-generated creative at speed; fast iteration and programmatic reach enable competitive advantage in cord-cutting households.

item.title: Oklahoma Governor Considers Special Session to Ban AI in Political Ads
item.description: Gov. Stitt considering special legislative session to ban AI in political advertising after deepfake attacks. First major state-level regulatory threat; campaigns must prepare state-by-state compliance strategies as cascade of AI ad bans likely by Q3 2026.

item.title: YouTube Expands AI Deepfake Detection Tool to All Creators over 18
item.description: YouTube rolling out likeness detection tool to all 18+ users, enabling anyone to detect and remove deepfakes. Platform enforcement on synthetic political content is accelerating; campaigns must move to disclosed synthetic media to survive detection and removal.

item.title: Political Money Is Flowing to Influencers. But From Whom?
item.description: Tom Steyer and other campaigns are paying influencers without proper FTC disclosures; California FPPC investigating. Campaigns must establish compliance protocols now to avoid enforcement action on emerging influencer targeting channel.

item.title: For 2026: Local TV Ad Trend Going To The 'Core'
item.description: Political TV ad spending surge (Nexstar +$6M YoY to $41M; Sinclair +$12M to $18M; Scripps up 173%) masks fundamental decline in core advertising and audience ratings. Political window is temporary; campaigns hedging with CTV and programmatic alternatives will outcompete local-only buyers post-midterms.
item.ns0:encoded: Political TV ad spending surge (Nexstar +$6M YoY to $41M; Sinclair +$12M to $18M; Scripps up 173%) masks fundamental decline in core advertising and audience ratings. Political window is temporary; campaigns hedging with CTV and programmatic alternatives will outcompete local-only buyers post-midterms.

item.title: Digital voter suppression ads tied to lower election turnout among specific demographic groups
item.description: PNAS study with real voting records: targeted digital suppression ads reduce minority voter turnout by 14 percentage points in battleground counties. Campaigns without defensive mobilization infrastructure and counter-suppression messaging will lose swing districts in 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: PNAS study with real voting records: targeted digital suppression ads reduce minority voter turnout by 14 percentage points in battleground counties. Campaigns without defensive mobilization infrastructure and counter-suppression messaging will lose swing districts in 2026.

item.title: House talks look at blocking some state AI laws, including in California and New York
item.description: Federal AI preemption legislation threatens state-level restrictions on AI in political advertising—campaigns scaling AI-powered ad creative will gain regulatory certainty advantage over state-compliant competitors. Table-stakes capability by November 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: Federal AI preemption legislation threatens state-level restrictions on AI in political advertising—campaigns scaling AI-powered ad creative will gain regulatory certainty advantage over state-compliant competitors. Table-stakes capability by November 2026.

item.title: Roku's Political Advertising Windfall: Masking a Platform Revenue Deceleration Nobody Wants to Mention
item.description: Political advertising spending is the primary growth driver for Roku Q1 2026; CTV is now the default platform for political campaigns. Streaming video inventory is tightening ahead of 2026 election. Campaigns that don't lock in CTV budgets by Q2 will face supply shortages and premium pricing in Q3-Q4. CTV CPMs for political content are rising 35-40% week-over-week.

item.title: YouTube's AI Deepfake Detection Tool Is Now Available To All Creators 18 And Older
item.description: YouTube is expanding deepfake detection to all adult users—a critical platform policy change that affects campaigns using AI-generated video content. Synthetic media on political ads now faces automated detection and removal. Campaigns relying on AI video must audit content and shift strategy, or face platform removal and reputational damage by October.
item.ns0:encoded: YouTube is expanding deepfake detection to all adult users—a critical platform policy change that affects campaigns using AI-generated video content. Synthetic media on political ads now faces automated detection and removal. Campaigns relying on AI video must audit content and shift strategy, or face platform removal and reputational damage by October.

item.title: I built a nonpartisan tracker for AI PAC spending in the 2026 midterms
item.description: Tech and AI companies have injected $185M into 2026 midterms through PAC networks backing 25 federal candidates. Policy agenda is deliberately separated from campaign messaging—making it difficult for political operatives to understand which candidates are AI-backed and what trade-offs come with that funding.

item.title: Political Money Is Flowing to Influencers. But From Whom?
item.description: Influencer payments for political endorsements are rising fast, but disclosure requirements remain murky. Campaigns are shifting budgets to influencer partnerships without traditional campaign finance transparency. Political operatives missing this emerging channel risk being out-reached by competitors who scale influencer networks.

item.title: Green signs bill to restrict corporate political spending
item.description: Hawaii Gov. Josh Green enacted Act 11, the first major state-level challenge to Citizens United by restricting corporate and Super PAC spending in elections. While legal challenges are expected, the law signals states are moving on campaign finance restrictions—affecting ad buyer strategies and Super PAC spending structures.
item.ns0:encoded: Hawaii Gov. Josh Green enacted Act 11, the first major state-level challenge to Citizens United by restricting corporate and Super PAC spending in elections. While legal challenges are expected, the law signals states are moving on campaign finance restrictions—affecting ad buyer strategies and Super PAC spending structures.

item.title: Democrats eye "hidden" Latino battlegrounds in 2026
item.description: New demographic modeling reveals Latino voters in GOP-drawn "safe" districts are reversing 2024 gains toward Republicans. Democrats are redirecting ad spend into competitive districts across Texas, California, New York, and Nevada—a strategic reallocation that signals major audience targeting shifts for 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: New demographic modeling reveals Latino voters in GOP-drawn "safe" districts are reversing 2024 gains toward Republicans. Democrats are redirecting ad spend into competitive districts across Texas, California, New York, and Nevada—a strategic reallocation that signals major audience targeting shifts for 2026.

item.title: The Steyer campaign pays influencers. Their posts don't always make that clear
item.description: Tom Steyer's gubernatorial campaign is under investigation for paying influencers without proper disclosure, violating California's 2023 influencer disclosure law. This marks the first major enforcement test for paid political influencer content—campaigns must now budget for compliance infrastructure or risk FPPC enforcement action.
item.ns0:encoded: Tom Steyer's gubernatorial campaign is under investigation for paying influencers without proper disclosure, violating California's 2023 influencer disclosure law. This marks the first major enforcement test for paid political influencer content—campaigns must now budget for compliance infrastructure or risk FPPC enforcement action.

item.title: AI-generated pro-Spencer Pratt mayoral campaign videos point to a new campaign tool
item.description: AI-generated videos supporting Spencer Pratt for LA mayor have gone viral, demonstrating how generative AI is becoming a mainstream campaign tool for creating compelling political narratives at scale. Campaign operators must now budget for AI content production alongside traditional creative.
item.ns0:encoded: AI-generated videos supporting Spencer Pratt for LA mayor have gone viral, demonstrating how generative AI is becoming a mainstream campaign tool for creating compelling political narratives at scale. Campaign operators must now budget for AI content production alongside traditional creative.

item.title: Traditional TV is trying to make ads more like online advertising
item.description: NBC, Fox, Warner Brothers, and other networks deploying agentic AI to buy/sell ads in real-time instead of upfront model—creating responsive, dynamic ad delivery that mirrors programmatic. Political campaigns can now adjust targeting and messaging at the same speed as digital platforms.

item.title: Congressional Campaign CTV Strategy: A District-Level CTV Playbook for the 2026 Cycle
item.description: CTV is now the anchor channel for 2026 House races (55–70% of TV spend), replacing broadcast as default; campaigns without district-level CTV targeting by Q3 2026 face structural disadvantage on persuasion efficiency, frequency optimization, and real-time creative velocity.

item.title: Digiday Programmatic Marketing Summit May Recap: How marketers are navigating agentic ad buying
item.description: Agentic AI ad buying is already live across brands and agencies; the competitive edge isn't the AI—it's the process guardrails and real-time decisioning velocity that separate winners from laggards in 2026 campaign cycles.
item.ns0:encoded: Agentic AI ad buying is already live across brands and agencies; the competitive edge isn't the AI—it's the process guardrails and real-time decisioning velocity that separate winners from laggards in 2026 campaign cycles.

item.title: Adform Opens Full-Stack Infrastructure for Agentic Advertising
item.description: Adform enables 800+ agentic capabilities across integrated platform; agencies can now interact directly through Claude, ChatGPT, or proprietary AI tools—fundamentally shifting how political media buyers access optimization, forecasting, and real-time campaign adjustments.

item.title: Marketers put up guardrails as AI agents reshape programmatic buying
item.description: AI agents in programmatic media buying are being adopted but heavily guardrailed by marketers concerned about budget autonomy and transparency. Campaigns deploying agentic AI must build governance frameworks with spend caps, human oversight, and decision logging to prevent runway spend and maintain control.
item.ns0:encoded: AI agents in programmatic media buying are being adopted but heavily guardrailed by marketers concerned about budget autonomy and transparency. Campaigns deploying agentic AI must build governance frameworks with spend caps, human oversight, and decision logging to prevent runway spend and maintain control.

item.title: By the numbers: Ad tech's quarter of mixed fortunes
item.description: Q1 2026 ad tech earnings show CTV is now 50%+ of spend on major DSPs and the only growth channel. Campaigns delaying CTV budget allocation will face inventory scarcity and premium pricing by Q3; budget reallocation decisions this quarter have direct Q4 performance impact.
item.ns0:encoded: Q1 2026 ad tech earnings show CTV is now 50%+ of spend on major DSPs and the only growth channel. Campaigns delaying CTV budget allocation will face inventory scarcity and premium pricing by Q3; budget reallocation decisions this quarter have direct Q4 performance impact.

item.title: Ad wars ramp up in D.C. mayoral race as primary enters final month
item.description: Two independent expenditure committees ($300k+ each) are running coordinated opposition against same candidate vulnerabilities—crime, utilities, ethics. Campaigns can now predict attack vectors and build counter-messaging in advance; pre-campaign opposition mapping is now essential for survival.
item.ns0:encoded: Two independent expenditure committees ($300k+ each) are running coordinated opposition against same candidate vulnerabilities—crime, utilities, ethics. Campaigns can now predict attack vectors and build counter-messaging in advance; pre-campaign opposition mapping is now essential for survival.

item.title: Outside cash floods U.S House runoffs in Texas
item.description: Outside groups now control 89% of ad spend in Texas congressional runoffs—up from 29% in 2020. Campaigns running independent of outside spending cannot compete; rapid-response to external messaging and coalition coordination are now table stakes for 2026.
item.ns0:encoded: Outside groups now control 89% of ad spend in Texas congressional runoffs—up from 29% in 2020. Campaigns running independent of outside spending cannot compete; rapid-response to external messaging and coalition coordination are now table stakes for 2026.
